hints.text: Bollards: most have an **off-centred reflector** and are flat (no depth)
**B-Profile guardrails** used exclusively (North Macedonia does not use them)
Chevrons = **black arrow on white** (also Slovenia/Montenegro/N. Macedonia)
Plate white + blue strip left; **agricultural plates green** (Croatia/N. Macedonia usually have no strip)
**Serbian** uses both Cyrillic & Latin; unique **Ђ Ћ**; in Latin: Č Ć Đ Š Ž
4 different **truck colours** by area; smallcam; Gen3 mostly **no antenna** (rare for central/E. Europe)
Coverage mostly **Vojvodina (north)**, around **Belgrade**, and **Niš (SE)**; Kosovo has none
2-digit area codes (first digit 1–3): **2 = north, 1 = central/east, 3 = SW**
**Vojvodina** (north) = extremely flat farmland (Fruška Gora ridge is the exception); rolling hills centre; mountains SW/SE (Dinaric/Balkan)
**Vršac Mountains** protrude from flat land near Romania (vineyards + castle); **Hungarian bilingual** signs → northern Vojvodina
